sentences.sentence: Like their modern relatives, mammoths were quite large.
sentences.translation: 和它们的现代近亲一样，猛犸象体型相当庞大。
difficult_points: Like 是介词，后接名词短语表达比较，不引导从句。
their modern relatives 指现代象类。

sentences.sentence: The largest known species reached heights in the region of 4 m at the shoulder and weights of up to 8 tonnes, while exceptionally large males may have exceeded 12 tonnes.
sentences.translation: 已知最大的物种肩高约4米、体重可达8吨，而体型特别大的雄性个体可能超过12吨。

structure.text: reached heights in the region of 4 m at the shoulder and weights of up to 8 tonnes
while exceptionally large males may have exceeded 12 tonnes

difficult_points: known 是过去分词作定语；reached 后并列 heights 和 weights 两个宾语。
may have exceeded 是情态动词加完成式，表示对过去体重的推测。

sentences.sentence: However, most species of mammoth were only about as large as a modern Asian elephant.
sentences.translation: 不过，大多数猛犸象物种的体型只与现代亚洲象大致相当。

structure.text: were only about as large as a modern Asian elephant

difficult_points: as large as 比较猛犸象与亚洲象的体型。
only about 限定比较只是大致相当，并纠正前句极端个体造成的印象。

sentences.sentence: Both sexes bore tusks.
sentences.translation: 雌雄猛犸象都长有长牙。
difficult_points: Both sexes 指雄性和雌性。
bore 是 bear 的过去式，在此意为生有、长有。

sentences.sentence: A first, small set appeared at about the age of six months, and these were replaced at about 18 months by the permanent set.
sentences.translation: 第一副较小的长牙约在六个月大时长出，并在约18个月大时被永久牙替换。

structure.text: at about the age of six months

difficult_points: set 在此指一副长牙；these 回指 A first, small set，形式上为复数指其中的牙。
were replaced by 是被动结构，说明永久牙替代乳牙。

sentences.sentence: Growth of the permanent set was at a rate of about 2.5 to 15.2 cm per year.
sentences.translation: 永久长牙每年的生长速度约为2.5至15.2厘米。

structure.text: was at a rate of about 2.5 to 15.2 cm per year

difficult_points: Growth 是主语中心词，of 短语说明生长对象。
at a rate of 表示按某一速率，per year 表示每年。

sentences.sentence: Based on studies of their close relatives, the modern elephants, mammoths probably had a gestation period of 22 months, resulting in a single calf being born.
sentences.translation: 根据对其近亲现代象的研究推测，猛犸象的妊娠期可能为22个月，每胎产下一只幼崽。

structure.text: Based on studies of their close relatives, the modern elephants
mammoths probably had a gestation period of 22 months
resulting in a single calf being born

difficult_points: Based on ... 表示推断依据；the modern elephants 与 their close relatives 同位。
resulting in 表示结果，being born 是动名词的被动形式，a single calf 是其逻辑主语。

sentences.sentence: Their social structure was probably the same as that of African and Asian elephants, with females living in herds headed by a matriarch, whilst bulls lived solitary lives or formed loose groups after sexual maturity.
sentences.translation: 它们的社会结构可能与非洲象和亚洲象相同：雌性群居，由一头母象带领；雄性则独居，或在性成熟后组成松散群体。

structure.text: Their social structure was probably the same as that of African and Asian elephants
whilst bulls lived solitary lives or formed loose groups

difficult_points: that 代替 social structure，避免重复。
with + 名词 + 现在分词描述雌性群体状态；headed by ... 修饰 herds。
whilst 对比雌性与雄性的社会生活，lived 和 formed 是并列谓语。

sentences.sentence: MEXICO CITY – Although it's hard to imagine in this age of urban sprawl and automobiles, North America once belonged to mammoths, camels, ground sloths as large as cows, bear-size beavers and other formidable beasts.
sentences.translation: 墨西哥城——在这个城市无序扩张、汽车遍地的时代，虽然很难想象，但北美曾是猛犸象、骆驼、牛一般大的地懒、熊一般大的河狸以及其他庞然猛兽的天下。

structure.text: Although it's hard to imagine in this age of urban sprawl and automobiles
North America once belonged to mammoths, camels, ground sloths as large as cows, bear-size beavers and other formidable beasts

difficult_points: MEXICO CITY 是新闻报道地点标记，破折号后才是正文。
Although 从句中 it 是形式主语，实际难以想象的是后面的主句事实。
belonged to 是比喻，表示这些动物曾主宰北美。

sentences.sentence: Some 11,000 years ago, however, these large-bodied mammals and others – about 70 species in all – disappeared.
sentences.translation: 然而，大约1.1万年前，这些大型哺乳动物及其他动物——总计约70个物种——消失了。
difficult_points: Some 放在数字前表示“大约”。
破折号间的 about 70 species in all 是插入说明，对主语范围作补充。

sentences.sentence: Their demise coincided roughly with the arrival of humans in the New World and dramatic climatic change – factors that have inspired several theories about the die-off.
sentences.translation: 它们的灭亡大致与人类抵达新大陆和剧烈气候变化发生在同一时期——这两个因素催生了多种关于大灭绝的理论。

structure.text: coincided roughly with the arrival of humans in the New World and dramatic climatic change
that have inspired several theories about the die-off

difficult_points: coincide with 只表示时间上大致同时，并不直接证明因果。
factors 与前面两件事同位，that 从句修饰 factors。

sentences.sentence: Yet despite decades of scientific investigation, the exact cause remains a mystery.
sentences.translation: 然而，尽管科学界调查了数十年，确切原因仍是谜团。
difficult_points: despite 后接名词短语表达让步，不是从句。
remain 为系动词，a mystery 作表语。

sentences.sentence: Now new findings offer support to one of these controversial hypotheses: that human hunting drove this megafaunal menagerie to extinction.
sentences.translation: 如今，新发现为其中一个有争议的假说提供了支持：人类捕猎导致这群大型动物灭绝。

structure.text: offer support to one of these controversial hypotheses
that human hunting drove this megafaunal menagerie to extinction

difficult_points: 冒号后的 that 从句是 hypothesis 的同位语，说明假说内容。
drive ... to extinction 表示导致某物种灭绝；menagerie 在此泛指一群大型动物。

sentences.sentence: The overkill model emerged in the 1960s, when it was put forth by Paul S. Martin of the University of Arizona.
sentences.translation: “过度猎杀”模型出现于20世纪60年代，当时由亚利桑那大学的保罗·S·马丁提出。

structure.text: The overkill model emerged in the 1960s
when it was put forth by Paul S. Martin of the University of Arizona

difficult_points: 源文在 University of 与 Arizona 之间有换行，句子内容应按同一句保留并规范为空格。
when 从句修饰 the 1960s；put forth 是被动语态，表示理论被提出。

sentences.sentence: Since then, critics have charged that no evidence exists to support the idea that the first Americans hunted to the extent necessary to cause these extinctions.
sentences.translation: 此后，批评者一直指称，没有证据支持这样一种观点：最早的美洲居民曾进行足以导致这些物种灭绝的大规模捕猎。

structure.text: that no evidence exists to support the idea
that the first Americans hunted to the extent necessary to cause these extinctions

difficult_points: 第一个 that 从句作 charged 的宾语，第二个 that 从句说明 idea 的内容。
to the extent necessary 表示达到必要程度，necessary 后的不定式说明何种程度。

sentences.sentence: But at the annual meeting of the Society of Vertebrate Paleontology in Mexico City last October, paleoecologist John Alroy of the University of California at Santa Barbara argued that, in fact, hunting-driven extinction is not only plausible, it was unavoidable.
sentences.translation: 但在去年10月于墨西哥城举行的脊椎动物古生物学会年会上，加州大学圣塔芭芭拉分校的古生态学家约翰·阿尔罗伊主张，事实上，由捕猎导致的灭绝不仅合理可信，而且不可避免。

structure.text: at the annual meeting of the Society of Vertebrate Paleontology in Mexico City last October
paleoecologist John Alroy of the University of California at Santa Barbara
that, in fact, hunting-driven extinction is not only plausible, it was unavoidable

difficult_points: that 从句作 argued 的宾语；in fact 是插入语。
原文 not only plausible, it was unavoidable 缺少标准并列连词 but also，且 is/was 时态不一致，应原样保留并按递进理解。

sentences.sentence: He has determined, using a computer simulation, that even a very modest amount of hunting would have wiped these animals out.
sentences.translation: 他通过计算机模拟得出结论：即使捕猎量非常有限，也足以使这些动物灭绝。

structure.text: that even a very modest amount of hunting would have wiped these animals out

difficult_points: using ... 是插入的现在分词短语，说明得出结论的方法。
would have wiped ... out 表示模型推演中的过去假设结果；wipe out 意为彻底消灭。

sentences.sentence: Assuming an initial human population of 100 people that grew no more than 2 percent annually, Alroy determined that if each band of, say, 50 people killed 15 to 20 large mammals a year, humans could have eliminated the animal populations within 1,000 years.
sentences.translation: 阿尔罗伊假设最初人口为100人、年增长率不超过2%，据此判断：如果每个约50人的群体每年猎杀15至20只大型哺乳动物，人类就可能在1000年内消灭这些动物种群。

structure.text: Assuming an initial human population of 100 people
that grew no more than 2 percent annually
that if each band of, say, 50 people killed 15 to 20 large mammals a year
humans could have eliminated the animal populations within 1,000 years

difficult_points: Assuming ... 表示模型前提，that 从句修饰 population。
determined 后的 that 从句内部又含 if 条件句；say 是插入语，表示“比如说、大约”。
could have eliminated 表示在设定条件下可能发生的过去结果。

sentences.sentence: Large mammals in particular would have been vulnerable to the pressure because they have longer gestation periods than smaller mammals and their young require extended care.
sentences.translation: 大型哺乳动物尤其容易受到这种压力影响，因为它们的妊娠期比小型哺乳动物更长，而且幼崽需要更长时间的照料。

structure.text: would have been vulnerable to the pressure
because they have longer gestation periods than smaller mammals
and their young require extended care

difficult_points: would have been 表示模型情境下的推断；the pressure 指人类捕猎压力。
because 后有两个并列原因，their young 中 young 是名词，指幼崽。

sentences.sentence: Not everyone agrees with Alroy's assessment.
sentences.translation: 并非所有人都同意阿尔罗伊的判断。
difficult_points: Not everyone 是部分否定，意为并非人人，而不是无人同意。
assessment 指阿尔罗伊关于捕猎必然导致灭绝的模型判断。

sentences.sentence: For one, the results depend in part on population-size estimates for the extinct animals – figures that are not necessarily reliable.
sentences.translation: 首先，这些结果部分依赖于对灭绝动物种群规模的估算——而这些数字并不一定可靠。

structure.text: depend in part on population-size estimates for the extinct animals

difficult_points: For one 用于提出第一条反对理由。
figures 与 population-size estimates 同位，that 从句修饰 figures；not necessarily 表示不一定。

sentences.sentence: But a more specific criticism comes from mammalogist Ross D. E. MacPhee of the American Museum of Natural History in New York City, who points out that the relevant archaeological record contains barely a dozen examples of stone points embedded in mammoth bones (and none, it should be noted, are known from other megafaunal remains) – hardly what one might expect if hunting drove these animals to extinction.
sentences.translation: 但更具体的批评来自纽约美国自然历史博物馆的哺乳动物学家罗斯·D·E·麦克菲。他指出，相关考古记录中只有不足十几例石制尖器嵌在猛犸象骨骼中的证据（而且应当注意，其他大型动物遗骸中一例也没有）——这远非人们在捕猎导致这些动物灭绝的情况下所预期看到的证据。

structure.text: a more specific criticism comes from mammalogist Ross D. E. MacPhee of the American Museum of Natural History in New York City
that the relevant archaeological record contains barely a dozen examples of stone points embedded in mammoth bones
and none, it should be noted, are known from other megafaunal remains
if hunting drove these animals to extinction

difficult_points: who 修饰 MacPhee，that 从句作 points out 的宾语；embedded ... 修饰 stone points。
括号中 none 指此类石制尖器证据，it should be noted 是插入语。
破折号后的 hardly what ... 对证据数量作评价，if 从句给出捕猎假说成立时的预期。

sentences.sentence: Furthermore, some of these species had huge ranges – the giant Jefferson's ground sloth, for example, lived as far north as the Yukon and as far south as Mexico – which would have made slaughtering them in numbers sufficient to cause their extinction rather implausible, he says.
sentences.translation: 此外，有些物种的分布范围极广——例如巨型杰斐逊地懒，北至育空、南至墨西哥都有分布——他说，这会使人类大量猎杀它们并最终导致其灭绝的说法显得相当不可信。

structure.text: some of these species had huge ranges
the giant Jefferson's ground sloth, for example, lived as far north as the Yukon and as far south as Mexico
which would have made slaughtering them in numbers sufficient to cause their extinction rather implausible
slaughtering them in numbers sufficient to cause their extinction

difficult_points: 破折号内用地懒的南北分布举例说明 huge ranges。
which 回指物种分布范围广这一整件事；make + 宾语 + 形容词构成复合宾语。
slaughtering ... 是动名词短语作 made 的宾语，sufficient to ... 修饰 numbers。

expressions.text: as far north as ... and as far south as ...

sentences.sentence: MacPhee agrees that humans most likely brought about these extinctions (as well as others around the world that coincided with human arrival), but not directly.
sentences.translation: 麦克菲同意，这些灭绝事件很可能由人类造成（世界其他地区还有一些与人类到来同时发生的灭绝），但并非由人类直接造成。

structure.text: that humans most likely brought about these extinctions
as well as others around the world that coincided with human arrival

difficult_points: 第一个 that 从句作 agrees 的宾语；括号内 others 指其他灭绝事件。
but not directly 省略 humans brought them about，转向疾病间接传播假说。

sentences.sentence: Rather he suggests that people may have introduced hyperlethal disease, perhaps through their dogs or hitchhiking vermin, which then spread wildly among the immunologically naive species of the New World.
sentences.translation: 相反，他认为人类可能通过他们的狗或随行寄生害兽带来了致死性极强的疾病，随后疾病在新大陆缺乏免疫力的物种间迅速传播。

structure.text: that people may have introduced hyperlethal disease
perhaps through their dogs or hitchhiking vermin
which then spread wildly among the immunologically naive species of the New World

difficult_points: may have introduced 表示对过去事件的推测。
which 修饰 hyperlethal disease；immunologically naive 表示此前未接触病原、缺乏免疫力。
hitchhiking vermin 比喻随人类迁徙而来的害兽。

sentences.sentence: As in the overkill model, populations of large mammals would have a harder time recovering.
sentences.translation: 与过度猎杀模型中一样，大型哺乳动物种群会更难恢复。

structure.text: would have a harder time recovering

difficult_points: As in ... 是省略式比较，表示疾病模型与过度猎杀模型具有相同后果。
have a hard time doing 是固定结构，recovering 说明困难所在。

sentences.sentence: Repeated outbreaks of a hyperdisease could thus quickly drive them to the point of no return.
sentences.translation: 因此，这种超级致命疾病反复暴发，可能迅速把它们推向无法恢复的临界点。

structure.text: to the point of no return

difficult_points: them 指大型哺乳动物种群。
the point of no return 是固定比喻，指越过后便无法恢复的临界点。

expressions.text: drive ... to the point of no return
expressions.meaning_zh: 把……推向无法挽回的临界点

sentences.sentence: So far MacPhee does not have empirical evidence for the hyperdisease hypothesis, and it won't be easy to come by: hyperlethal disease would kill far too quickly to leave its signature on the bones themselves.
sentences.translation: 到目前为止，麦克菲尚无支持超级疾病假说的实证，而且这种证据也不容易获得：致死性极强的疾病杀死动物的速度太快，不会在骨骼本身留下痕迹。

structure.text: So far MacPhee does not have empirical evidence for the hyperdisease hypothesis
and it won't be easy to come by
hyperlethal disease would kill far too quickly
to leave its signature on the bones themselves

difficult_points: it 指 empirical evidence；come by 表示获得。
冒号后解释证据难找的原因；too quickly to leave ... 表示杀伤速度过快，来不及留下痕迹。

sentences.sentence: But he hopes that analyses of tissue and DNA from the last mammoths to perish will eventually reveal murderous microbes.
sentences.translation: 但他希望，对最后一批死亡猛犸象的组织和DNA进行分析，最终能揭示致命微生物的存在。

structure.text: that analyses of tissue and DNA from the last mammoths to perish will eventually reveal murderous microbes

difficult_points: that 从句作 hopes 的宾语，主语是 analyses。
to perish 修饰 the last mammoths，表示最后死亡的猛犸象。

sentences.sentence: The third explanation for what brought on this North American extinction does not involve human beings.
sentences.translation: 关于是什么引发北美这场灭绝的第三种解释，并不涉及人类。

structure.text: for what brought on this North American extinction
what brought on this North American extinction

difficult_points: what 从句作介词 for 的宾语，what 在从句中作 brought on 的主语。
bring on 表示引发，does not involve 引出纯气候解释。

sentences.sentence: Instead its proponents blame the loss on the weather.
sentences.translation: 相反，这一解释的支持者把灭绝归咎于气候。
difficult_points: its 指第三种解释，proponents 指该理论的支持者。
blame A on B 表示把 A 归因或归咎于 B。

sentences.sentence: The Pleistocene epoch witnessed considerable climatic instability, explains paleontologist Russell W. Graham of the Denver Museum of Nature and Science.
sentences.translation: 丹佛自然与科学博物馆的古生物学家拉塞尔·W·格雷厄姆解释说，更新世经历了相当剧烈的气候不稳定。

structure.text: explains paleontologist Russell W. Graham of the Denver Museum of Nature and Science

difficult_points: 主句先于报道语，explains 的主语是后置的人名。
epoch witnessed 是拟人化表达，意为该时期发生了气候不稳定。

sentences.sentence: As a result, certain habitats disappeared, and species that had once formed communities split apart.
sentences.translation: 结果，某些栖息地消失，曾经共同构成群落的物种彼此分离。
difficult_points: and 连接两个完整分句。
that 从句修饰 species，had formed 使用过去完成时，表示群落关系发生在分离之前。

sentences.sentence: For some animals, this change brought opportunity.
sentences.translation: 对一些动物而言，这种变化带来了机会。
difficult_points: this change 回指栖息地消失和物种群落分裂。
For some animals 限定影响对象，并为下句与大型动物的对比铺垫。

sentences.sentence: For much of the megafauna, however, the increasingly homogeneous environment left them with shrinking geographical ranges – a death sentence for large animals, which need large ranges.
sentences.translation: 然而，对许多大型动物来说，日益同质化的环境使它们的地理分布范围不断缩小——这对需要广阔活动范围的大型动物而言无异于死刑。

structure.text: left them with shrinking geographical ranges
a death sentence for large animals

difficult_points: leave somebody with something 表示使其处于某种结果，them 指 megafauna。
破折号后的 a death sentence 概括分布缩小的致命后果；which 修饰 large animals。

expressions.meaning_zh: 不断缩小的地理分布范围

sentences.sentence: Although these creatures managed to maintain viable populations through most of the Pleistocene, the final major fluctuation – the so-called Younger Dryas event – pushed them over the edge, Graham says.
sentences.translation: 格雷厄姆说，尽管这些动物在更新世的大部分时期都设法维持了可存续的种群，但最后一次重大波动——所谓的新仙女木事件——还是把它们推过了生存临界点。

structure.text: Although these creatures managed to maintain viable populations through most of the Pleistocene

difficult_points: Although 从句让步说明它们此前长期存活，主句强调最后波动的决定性影响。
破折号内是 fluctuation 的同位语；push ... over the edge 比喻越过生存临界点。

sentences.sentence: For his part, Alroy is convinced that human hunters demolished the titans of the Ice Age.
sentences.translation: 就阿尔罗伊而言，他确信是人类猎手消灭了冰河时代的巨兽。

structure.text: that human hunters demolished the titans of the Ice Age

difficult_points: be convinced that 表示确信某一观点；that 从句说明确信内容。
titans 是比喻，指冰河时代体型巨大的动物。

expressions.text: the titans of the Ice Age

sentences.sentence: The overkill model explains everything the disease and climate scenarios explain, he asserts, and makes accurate predictions about which species would eventually go extinct.
sentences.translation: 他断言，过度猎杀模型能够解释疾病和气候情景所能解释的一切，并能准确预测哪些物种最终会灭绝。

structure.text: the disease and climate scenarios explain
about which species would eventually go extinct

difficult_points: the disease ... explain 是省略 that 的定语从句，修饰 everything。
explains 和 makes 是并列谓语，he asserts 是插入报道语。
which 从句作介词 about 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: "Personally, I'm a vegetarian," he remarks, "and I find all of this kind of gross – but believable."
sentences.translation: “就我个人而言，我是素食主义者，”他评论道，“我觉得这一切有点令人反感——但确实可信。”

structure.text: and I find all of this kind of gross – but believable
find all of this kind of gross – but believable

difficult_points: 直接引语被报道语 he remarks 分隔，后半句由 and 继续。
find + 宾语 + 形容词构成复合宾语；gross 与 believable 由 but 对比。
